Class RKeYMetaConstructType

java.lang.Object
recoder.java.JavaSourceElement
recoder.java.JavaProgramElement
recoder.java.JavaNonTerminalProgramElement
recoder.java.reference.TypeReference
de.uka.ilkd.key.java.recoderext.RKeYMetaConstructType
All Implemented Interfaces:
KeYRecoderExtension, Serializable, Cloneable, ExpressionContainer, NamedProgramElement, NonTerminalProgramElement, ProgramElement, Reference, MemberReference, NameReference, PackageReferenceContainer, ReferencePrefix, ReferenceSuffix, TypeReferenceContainer, TypeReferenceInfix, SourceElement, ModelElement, NamedModelElement
public class RKeYMetaConstructType extends TypeReference implements KeYRecoderExtension

      Returns the number of children of this node.

      an int giving the number of children of this node

    • getChildAt

      public ProgramElement getChildAt(int index)
      Returns the child at the specified index in this node's "virtual" child array
      Specified by:
      getChildAt in interface NonTerminalProgramElement
      Overrides:
      getChildAt in class TypeReference
      Parameters:
      index - an index into this node's "virtual" child array
      Returns:
      the program element at the given position
      Throws:
      ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException - if index is out of bounds

    • getChildPositionCode

      public int getChildPositionCode(ProgramElement child0)
      Description copied from interface: NonTerminalProgramElement
      Returns the positional code of the given child, or -1 if there is no such child. The result contains an encoding of the relative position of the child as well as the role it has been playing in this parent element. This information is required internally for proper undo of transformations and is to be delivered to the detached method of the ChangeHistory.
      Specified by:
      getChildPositionCode in interface NonTerminalProgramElement
      Overrides:
      getChildPositionCode in class TypeReference
      Parameters:
      child0 - the exact child to look for.
      Returns:
      the positional code of the given child, or -1.
      See Also:
      • ChangeHistory.detached(ProgramElement, NonTerminalProgramElement, int)

    • getIndexOfChild

      public int getIndexOfChild(ProgramElement pe)
      Description copied from class: JavaNonTerminalProgramElement
      Returns the index of the given child, or -1 if there is no such child. The child is searched for by identity: getChildAt(getIndexOfChild(x)) == x.
      Specified by:
      getIndexOfChild in interface NonTerminalProgramElement
      Overrides:
      getIndexOfChild in class JavaNonTerminalProgramElement
      Parameters:
      pe - the exact child to look for.
      Returns:
      the index of the given child, or -1.

    • getIndexOfChild

      @Deprecated public int getIndexOfChild(int posCode)
      Deprecated.
      Description copied from class: JavaNonTerminalProgramElement
      Extracts the index of a child from its position code.

      This method does not return the child index as received by getIndexOfChild(), but rather the index within internal data structure representation.

      Therefore it is common that getIndexOfChild(getChildPositionCode(aChild)) != getIndexOfChild(aChild)

      This method is deprecated as of 0.75

      Specified by:
      getIndexOfChild in interface NonTerminalProgramElement
      Overrides:
      getIndexOfChild in class JavaNonTerminalProgramElement
      Parameters:
      posCode - the position code.
      Returns:
      the index of the given position code.
      See Also:
      • NonTerminalProgramElement.getChildPositionCode(ProgramElement)

    • getRoleOfChild

      public int getRoleOfChild(int i)
      Description copied from class: JavaNonTerminalProgramElement
      Extracts the role of a child from its position code.
      Specified by:
      getRoleOfChild in interface NonTerminalProgramElement
      Overrides:
      getRoleOfChild in class JavaNonTerminalProgramElement
      Parameters:
      i - the position code.
      Returns:
      the role code of the given position code.
      See Also:
      • NonTerminalProgramElement.getChildPositionCode(ProgramElement)
